{{Quote|I insist, Master Whie.|Fidelis offers to carry vacuum flowers for Whie Malreaux|<ref name="Y:DR" />}}
'''Vacuum flowers''' were a type of food served aboard the [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Reasonable Doubt]]''. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]] purchased five orders of vacuum flowers for him and his companions on board to eat. The food, however, ended up being dropped and went uneaten.

==Description==
Vacuum flowers were a food which was edible to [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Gran/Legends|Grans]] and [[Yoda's species/Legends|members]] of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda's]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Y:DR" />

==History==
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], vacuum flowers were served in the cafeteria onboard the [[Kut-Rate Kruise Lines]] companies' [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Reasonable Doubt]]''. While traveling to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]] on the ''Reasonable Doubt'', the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]] ordered vacuum flowers for him and his four Jedi companions on board, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Jai Maruk]] and his apprentice [[Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy]], both of whom were Human, Gran Master [[Maks Leem]] and Grand Master Yoda. He asked for five orders of vacuum flowers along with a
large serving of [[bubble-and-squirt]]; six [[Blasteroids!]]; [[double-fried chili dumpling]]s; a bucket of [[Crispy foot|crispy feet]]; a half bucket of extra gummy [[Rank (food)|rank]], and five drinks.<ref name="Y:DR" />

As he was suppose to be hiding his identity as a Jedi he decided he couldn't used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to help carry the food and instead accepted the help of a [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. The droid turned out to be [[Fidelis]]–a servant of [[House Malreaux]] of which Whie was part–and revealed [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] was at the family's home, [[Château Malreaux]], on Vjun. This information caused Malreaux to leave the cafeteria in order tell his companions and left Fidelis to carry all of the food. When the droid caught up with the padawan though, Yoda, who was disguised as an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]], bumped into him and caused all of the food to be dropped.<ref name="Y:DR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vacuum flowers first appeared in ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' a novel written by [[Sean Stewart]] and released in [[2004]]. While the book doesn't show the flowers being eaten by a Gran, Human or Yoda's species the dish is ordered for them and so this article assumes they could.

==Appearances==
*''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}}
